parameters.name: B3303 - Fuel Cut-off Limiters in Gear (RPM)
parameters.description:
This table configures the Fuel Cut-off Rev Limiter - when the transmission is in Gear.

*** This is the main Rev Limiter ***

Cut-off = when RPM reaches this value, Fuel will be cut

Enable = af

parameters.name: B3305 - Cold Engine Protection Timer
parameters.description: The PCM reads the Engine Coolant Temperature (ECT) at Startup, then based on that temperature, this table determines how long the Cold Engine RPM Limiters will be used.

parameters.name: B3306 - Cold Engine RPM Limiter
parameters.description:
If the PCM has determined the engine is in a cold condition, based on Engine Coolant Temperature (ECT) at startup, this is the RPM Limiter Table that will be used.

*** This is the main Rev Limiter

parameters.name: B3648 - Commanded Fuel When in Open Loop (Ethanol)
parameters.description:
When the engine is in Cold Open Loop Mode, this is the Commanded Fuel (Lambda).

Column headings = ECT (F)
Row headings = MAP

parameters.name: B3660 - Open Loop Ethanol Blend Factor
parameters.description: Blends B3605 - Commanded Fuel When in Open Loop (Normal), and B3648 - Commanded Fuel When in Open Loop (Ethanol) - based on % Ethanol content in the fuel being used.

parameters.name: B3701 - Injector Pulse Width Voltage Adjustment
parameters.description:
As Battery Voltage varies, the Injector Pulse Widths (IPW) are adjusted by this value (milliseconds), to compensate for the changes in the opening and closing times.

Column headings = MAP
Row head

parameters.name: B3704 - Injector Bank Assignments
parameters.description:
Assigns each Injector output to Bank 1 or Bank 2, of the Engine.

0 = Bank 1
1 = Bank 2
2 = None (i.e. the particular Injector Assignment is not in use)

parameters.name: B4001 - Injector Flow Rate (Grams/sec)
parameters.description: Flow Rate (grams per second) of the Fuel Injectors.  If the Fuel Rail Pressure is NOT referenced to Manifold Pressure (Returnless style), then decreasing the Manifold Pressure has the same effect as i

parameters.name: B4002 - Injector Voltage Correction
parameters.description: Injector Flow compensation based on Fuel Pump Voltage.

parameters.name: B4003 - Minimum Injector Pulse Width (Milliseconds)
parameters.description: This is the Minimum Injector Pulse Width (milliseconds) the PCM can command for a given RPM.

parameters.name: B4004 - Defaullt Minimum Injector Pulse Width (Milliseconds)
parameters.description: If the PCM commands a Injector Pulse, which is less than Minimum Injector Pulse Width (B4003), then the Injector Pulse Times (milliseconds) from this table will be used instead.

parameters.name: B4304 - Startup Airflow Correction
parameters.description: Correction to Airflow on startup - referenced to Engine Coolant Temperature (ECT). This value is added to Startup Friction Airflow Correction (B4343) for a final airflow correction value on startup.

parameters.name: B4307 - Desired Airflow (grams/sec)
parameters.description:
Used to reduce Airflow as the engine warms up - referenced to Engine Coolant Temperature (ECT).

While primarily used to configure the Idle Airflow, this parameter also controls the Base Airflow for

parameters.name: B4308 - Airflow Parked
parameters.description:
When the IAC Motor is in the Parked position this is the required Airflow.  The IAC will be moved to the Parked position in situations like engine shutdown and key on.

This table is referenced to I

parameters.name: B4309 - Throttle Cracker Airflow in Gear (grams/sec)
parameters.description:
Airflow values added to Idle Airflow when the Throttle Cracker function is active and the transmission is in Gear.

Column headings = RPM
Row headings = Vehicle Speed (KPH)

If both the Throttle

parameters.name: B4310 - Throttle Cracker Airflow in P/N (grams/sec)
parameters.description:
Airflow values added to Idle Airflow when the Throttle Cracker function is active and the transmission is in Park or Neutral (or the clutch is depressed).

If both the Throttle Cracker Airflow and t

parameters.name: B4313 - Throttle Cracker Decay Rate
parameters.description:
When the Throttle Cracker is deactivated, the Throttle Cracker Airflow value is decayed by this amount every 100ms.

Also see:
Throttle Cracker Deactivate Speed (B4312)

parameters.name: B4314 - Throttle Cracker Decay Delay
parameters.description:
When the Throttle Cracker is to be deactivated, the PCM will wait this long before doing so.

Also see:
Throttle Cracker Deactivate Speed (B4312)

parameters.name: B4315 - Throttle Follower Airflow
parameters.description: Airflow values added to Idle Airflow when the Throttle Follower function is active.  This value is multiplied by Throttle Follower Airflow RPM Multiplier (B4316) to obtain the final Throttle Follower

parameters.name: B4316 - Throttle Follower Airflow RPM Multiplier
parameters.description:
The Airflow values from Throttle Follower Airflow (B4315) are multiplied by this value to obtain the final Throttle Follower Airflow value.

If both the Throttle Cracker Airflow and the Throttle Fol

parameters.name: B4317 - Throttle Follower Decay Rate in Gear (grams/sec)
parameters.description:
When the transmission is in Gear, the Throttle Follower Airflow value is decayed by this amount every 12.5ms.

Column headings = Gear
Row headings = Vehicle Speed (KPH)

Also see:
Throttle Follo

parameters.name: B4318 - Throttle Follower Decay Delay in Gear
parameters.description:
When the Throttle Follower Airflow is to be decayed and the transmission is in Gear, the PCM will wait this long before starting the decay.

Also see:
Throttle Follower TPS change (B4321)

parameters.name: B4319 - Throttle Follower Decay Rate in P/N
parameters.description:
When the transmission is in Park or Neutral (or the clutch is depressed), the Throttle Follower Airflow value is decayed by this amount every 12.5ms.

Also see:
Throttle Follower TPS change (B4321)

parameters.name: B4320 - Throttle Follower Decay Delay in P/N
parameters.description:
When the Throttle Follower Airflow is to be decayed and the transmission is in Park or Neutral (or the clutch is depressed), the PCM will wait this long before starting the decay.

Also see:
Thrott

parameters.name: B4332 - Rolling Idle RPM
parameters.description: The desired Idle Speed (RPM) when the vehicle is not stationary, and the Automatic Transmission is in Neutral, or with a Manual Transmission the Clutch is depressed.  Rolling desired Idle Mode may onl

parameters.name: B4336 - Rolling Idle High Correction
parameters.description:
Rolling Idle Airflow correction (grams/sec) when RPM is too High.

Also see:
Rolling Idle Disable Speed (B4333)
Rolling Idle Enable Speed (B4334)
Rolling Idle Maximum TPS Position (B4335)

parameters.name: B4337 - Rolling Idle Low Correction
parameters.description:
Rolling Idle Airflow correction (grams/sec) when RPM is too Low.

Also see:
Rolling Idle Disable Speed (B4333)
Rolling Idle Enable Speed (B4334)
Rolling Idle Maximum TPS Position (B4335)

parameters.name: B4338 - Engine Speed Compensation (grams/sec)
parameters.description:
Extra Airflow required to compensate for the load placed on the engine by external loads such as Initial startup, Alternator, or Heater.

Column headings = In Gear or In P/N (Park or Neutral)
Row h

parameters.name: B4339 - Engine Speed Compensation Multiplier
parameters.description: This table is a Multiplier (Factor) for the Engine Speed Compensation (B4338) table.  It may be used to modify the Engine Speed Compensation (grams/sec) at various Engine Coolant Temperatures (ECT).

parameters.name: B4342 - Startup Airflow Delay (Camshaft Revolutions)
parameters.description:
Startup Idle Airflow Correction begins to decay after this many Camshaft Revolutions.  This parameter is referenced to Engine Coolant Temperature (ECT).

Also see:
Startup Airflow Correction (B4304

parameters.name: B4343 - Startup Friction Airflow Correction (ECT)
parameters.description: Correction to Idle Airflow on Startup to overcome initial starting friction and cold engine components - referenced to Engine Coolant Temperature (ECT).  This value is added to Startup Airflow Correct

parameters.name: B4344 - Startup Friction Airflow Decay (Crankshaft Revolutions)
parameters.description:
Startup Friction Airflow correction is decayed by this amount, every 90 degrees of Crankshaft Rotation.  This parameter is referenced to Engine Coolant Temperature (ECT).

Also see:
Startup Frictio

parameters.name: B4345 - Startup Friction Airflow Delay (Camshaft Revolutions)
parameters.description:
Startup Friction Airflow correction begins to decay after this many Camshaft Revolutions.  This parameter is referenced to Engine Coolant Temperature (ECT).

Also see:
Startup Friction Airflow Corr

parameters.name: B4354 - A/C Airflow Ramp In (grams/sec)
parameters.description: Used to Ramp In the IAC Airflow Compensation for the torque load when the A/C Compressor Clutch is engaged.

parameters.name: B4355 - A/C Airflow Ramp Out (grams/sec)
parameters.description: Used to Ramp Out the IAC Airflow Compensation for the torque load when the A/C Compressor Clutch is disengaged.

parameters.name: B4403 - IAC Effective Area (Steps)
parameters.description: Used to convert Effective Area (which the PCM calculates from the Idle Airflow) into IAC Motor Steps.

parameters.name: B4512 - Filtered RPM Airflow Correction (grams/sec)
parameters.description:
Airflow Correction to be applied based on RPM Rate of Change which attempts to dampen overshoot of the Desired RPM.

Column headings = RPM Increasing (+ RPM) or RPM Decreasing (- RPM)
Row headings

parameters.name: B4514 - Learned Airflow Correction (grams/sec)
parameters.description:
Correction to Learned Airflow when Engine Speed is too High or too Low and transmission is in Gear or Park/Neutral (P/N).  This table is referenced to Absolute RPM Error (the Row headings).

Absolut

parameters.name: B4515 - Direct Airflow Correction (grams/sec)
parameters.description:
Correction to Direct Airflow when Engine Speed is too High or too Low under various transmission and A/C conditions.  This table is referenced to Absolute RPM Error (the Row headings).

Absolute RPM

parameters.name: B4521 - Maximum Idle Short Term Correction
parameters.description:
This is the Maximum Correction the Short Term Idle Learn may adjust.

This parameter is referenced to Engine Coolant Temperature (ECT).

parameters.name: B4522 - Stall Saver RPM
parameters.description:
Engine Speed must fall below the table value to initiate Stall Saver Mode.

Column headings = in Gear or in P/N (Park or Neutral)
Row headings = RPM

As an example, if the column value is 500 RPM

parameters.name: B4603 - Desired Idle Speeds
parameters.description:
Desired Idle Speeds.  Based on various Transmission and Air Conditioner combinations.  This parameter is referenced to Engine Coolant Temperature (ECT) - which are the Row headings.

Column headings

parameters.name: B4604 - Startup RPM Offset
parameters.description:
Upon engine Startup this is the RPM Offset which is added to the values in Desired Idle Speeds (B4603).  This parameter is referenced to Engine Coolant Temperature (ECT).

Column headings = in Gear

parameters.name: B4605 - Heater Warmup RPM Offset
parameters.description: In an attempt to get the engine hotter faster and increase the heater temperature this is the RPM Offset which is added to the values in Desired Idle Speeds (B4603).  This parameter is referenced to E

parameters.name: B5401 - ETC Controlled RPM Limit
parameters.description:
This table configures the Electronic Throttle Control (ETC) Rev Limiter

*** This is the main Rev Limiter for Drive-By-Wire vehicles***

parameters.name: B5934 - Idle Flare Control (Degrees)
parameters.description: This table makes adjustments to Spark in an attempt to curb Idle Speed Flare upon Start-up.

parameters.name: B5935 - Idle Overspeed Error
parameters.description: If the actual Idle Speed is above the desired Idle Speed value, then this table is used to make adjustments to Spark Timing.  The RPM Error values are in relation to the difference between actual Idle

parameters.name: B5936 - Idle Underspeed Error
parameters.description: If the actual Idle Speed is below the desired Idle Speed value, then this table is used to make adjustments to Spark Timing.  The RPM Error values are in relation to the difference between actual Idle

parameters.name: B5947 - Ethanol Spark Advance Correction (Degrees)
parameters.description:
This table is used to alter (correct) the Spark Advance, when Ethanol (E85) is being used as Fuel.

Column headings = RPM
Row headings = Grams/Cylinder

parameters.name: B5948 - Burst Knock Timing (Degrees)
parameters.description: Burst Knock will retard or advance timing upon detection of a large change in Intake Air Mass.  If the change in Air Mass exceeds the values in the Burst Knock Delta Air Mass (B6210) table, then the v

parameters.name: B5954 - Burst Knock Delta Air Mass
parameters.description:
When a change in Cylinder Air Mass exceeds these calibrated values, then Burst Knock can be Enabled.

Also see:
Burst Knock Retard (B6212)

parameters.name: B6202 - Knock Fast Attack Gain (Scaler)
parameters.description:
This table sets the Gain or adjustment range of the Knock System, dependent on Engine Coolant Temperature (ECT).

e.g.:
To allow full knock retard adjustments, set to 1.00.
To disable set to 0.00.

parameters.name: B6203 - Knock Fast Attack Rate
parameters.description:
Once Knock is detected, the Timing will be reduced by this many Degrees per Knock Sensor Volt, every Low Resolution Reference Pulse (4x per crank rotation for V8, 3x for V6).

Also see:
Knock Fast

parameters.name: B6204 - Knock Retard Limit Not in PE Mode (Degrees)
parameters.description: If not in Power Enrichment (PE) Mode, then Knock can only retard the timing by these values.

parameters.name: B6205 - Knock Retard Limit When in PE Mode (Degrees)
parameters.description: If in Power Enrichment (PE) Mode, then Knock can only retard the timing by these values.

parameters.name: B6244 - Knock Sensor Multiplier A (Cylinder Mode)
parameters.description: This Multiplier is applied when not in RPM Knock Mode or TPS Knock Mode, otherwise the appropriate RPM or TPS Knock Multipliers are applied.  This value is Multiplied with the Learned Knock Threshold

parameters.name: B6245 - Knock Sensor Multiplier B (Cylinder Mode)
parameters.description:
This Multiplier is always applied when Knock Detection is in Cylinder Mode.

Higher values desensitize the Knock Sensors.
Lower values Sensitize the Knock Sensors.

Column headings = MAP (kPa)
R

parameters.name: B6250 - Knock Sensor TPS Multiplier
parameters.description:
This Multiplier is applied when in TPS Knock Mode.  TPS Knock Mode is active when all three TPS conditions are met - Minimum, Maximum, and Delta.

This value is Multiplied with the Learned Knock Thr

parameters.name: B6255 - Knock Sensor RPM Multiplier
parameters.description:
This Multiplier is applied when in RPM Knock Mode.  RPM Knock Mode is active when all three RPM conditions are met - Minimum, Maximum, and Delta.

This value is Multiplied with the Learned Knock Thr

parameters.name: B6605 - % Torque Loss from Spark Retard (Degrees)
parameters.description: Amount of Spark Retard (degrees) required to cause a Torque Lost (%) from the Traction Control System (TCS) Spark Retard.  This table tells the PCM how much Spark Retard (degrees) is required to cause

parameters.name: B6611 - Max Torque by Gear (Nm)
parameters.description:
This table is used to determine the Maximum Torque (Nm) allowed by Gear. The Traction Control System (TCS) will use these values to reduce the Engine Torque.

Column headings = Gear
Row headings =

parameters.name: F0501 - Fuel Gauge Calibration
parameters.description: This table determines the Pulse Width Modulation (PWM) for a Fuel Gauge that is driven directly from the PCM.  If this table is zeroed in your stock tune, then assume the PCM does not control the Fuel
